Georgia (modern borders) — wine's 8,000-year origin
description: Modern political border (post-1991 independence). ORIGIN of wine — 8,000-year-old qvevri clay vessels at Gadachrili Gora near Tbilisi. Traditional qvevri method recognised by UNESCO 2013.

France + Italy + Spain (modern borders) — 'Old World' wine core
description: Modern political borders. France (Bordeaux, Burgundy, Champagne, Loire, Rhône). Italy (Tuscany, Piedmont, Veneto). Spain (Rioja, Ribera del Duero). Together produce ~50% of world wine and hold the defining appellation systems.

Argentina + Chile (modern borders) — Malbec + Carmenere revival
description: Modern political borders. Mendoza Argentina rescued Malbec from extinction in Europe. Chile's Central Valley preserved pre-phylloxera European vines. Together #5 + #8 world producers.
